text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little village, there lived a clever owl named Oliver. Although small in stature, Oliver possessed an immense intellect. He knew every bird proverb ever uttered. He could solve any puzzle. He could spin elaborate stories.

One afternoon, as golden sunlight filled his dormitory attic home, Oliver heard rustling outside. Curious, he fluttered out. There were squirrels! They were chasing something shiny. Oliver swooped down. He caught a silver ribbon. It belonged to Owl City's treasure chest.

Back home, he tied the ribbon tight. That evening, Owl City came. He showed off his find. The owl king appreciated Oliver's cleverness. He rewarded him with feathers.

Every night thereafter, Oliver decorated Owl City. He painted stars. He hung nets. He placed tiny bells. The forest enjoyed dances under his stars. Birds sang. Owl City became a wonderland.

But Oliver longed for something more. He wanted friends. Friends who weren.
